channel.title: CarPartsRadar Buying Guides
channel.description: Independent automotive fitment and parts-buying guidance.

item.title: How to Confirm a Car Part Fits Before You Buy
item.description: A practical fitment checklist using the VIN, trim, engine, drivetrain, production date, and manufacturer part numbers.

item.title: OEM vs. Aftermarket Car Parts: How to Choose
item.description: Compare original equipment, aftermarket, remanufactured, and used parts by fit, warranty, cost, and repair risk.

item.title: Brake Pad Buying Guide: Fit, Material, and Total Cost
item.description: Choose replacement brake pads by axle, caliper package, friction material, hardware, driving conditions, and rotor condition.

item.title: Alternator Buying Guide: Output, Fitment, and Core Charges
item.description: Compare alternators by rated output, connector, pulley, mounting, remanufacturing quality, warranty, and core-return cost.

item.title: Starter Motor Buying Guide: Diagnose First, Then Match
item.description: Select a starter by engine, transmission, tooth count, rotation, mounting, electrical terminals, warranty, and core policy.

item.title: Oxygen Sensor Buying Guide: Bank, Position, and Connector
item.description: Identify the correct upstream or downstream oxygen sensor by bank, sensor number, emissions package, connector, and original part number.

item.title: Shocks vs. Struts: What to Check Before Ordering
item.description: Understand shock and strut applications, complete assemblies, electronic suspension options, axle pairing, and alignment costs.

item.title: How to Compare the Real Cost of an Online Car Part
item.description: Calculate delivered price, shipping, tax, core deposits, hardware, programming, returns, downtime, and warranty risk.
